lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh, thank Armadyl! I am in such a worry... please help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With what?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh... it is terrible... horrible... My boyfriend lives here in this lighthouse, but I haven't seen him the last few days! I think something terrible has happened!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Look, you can see for yourself that the light has gone out, and the front door is locked up tight! He would NEVER do that!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: With the light off this coastline is terribly dangerous to ships! And to lock the front door so that nobody can turn the light back on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe he just went on holiday or something? Must be pretty boring living in a lighthouse.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: That is terribly irresponsible! He is far too thoughtful for that! He would never leave it unattended! He would also never leave without telling me!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Please, I know something terrible has happened to him... I can sense it! Please... please help me adventurer!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how can I help?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Well, we have to do something to get the lighthouse working again! Also, as you may have noticed, the storm that knocked the bridge out

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: has trapped me on this causeway! You seem to have got here okay somehow, so if you could go and visit my cousin and get the spare key I left him,

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: as well as fix the bridge enough so that I can go and speak to my family in Rellekka and tell them whats happened, I will be eternally grateful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll help!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: OH! THANK YOU SO MUCH! I know my darling would never have left with the lighthouse lights off and without even telling me where he's gone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is your cousin?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: My cousin was always interested in agility. He left our home in Rellekka many moons ago, so that he could pursue this interest.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I don't exactly know where he has gone, but I am sure he went somewhere to practise his agility. If you see him, his name is Gunnjorn. Mention my name, he will recognise it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I fix the bridge?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Well, I am not just some helpless girl! I have pretty good agility, so you will only need to use two planks to make a ledge that I can balance along.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Just use a plank on each side of the bridge. You will need a hammer, and thirty steel nails for each plank you use as well. I believe there are some planks near here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Thank you so much!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, just passing through.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh... my poor darling Jossik... I just know something terrible has happened...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, just passing through.

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh... my poor darling Jossik... I just know something terrible has happened...

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Oh! Thank you so much for all of your help in rescuing my darling Jossik!

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I do not know what would have happened to him had you not chanced along this path!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry about it, I was glad to help out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any more paint?

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: Certainly. How much do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I have something you might be interested in...

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: A book about lighthouses? I love it! I actually have something you might be interested in as well...

lines.speaker: Larrissa
lines.text: I have way too much of that paint for the lighthouse. If you want more of it, just ask!
